comments.body: @cevich Can we get failed tests to run multiple times on failures.  Seems like Humans are rerunning the same tests over and over again.  Especially the Ubuntu and RHEL7 tests.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *baude*, *jwhonce* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [baude] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@rhatdan ginkgo does this (to a degree) for free (the `-flateattempts 3` option).  I get what you're saying though.  RHEL should go away when RHEL testing goes away.  The Ubuntu "pod on pod top" (or something) is a real (honest) failure of some sort.  Re-running it automatically won't fix anything, it'll just make people wait longer for results that accidentally passed :frowning_face: (hiding the problem)

Chatted with @baude and @mheon about this on Monday night.  I'm afraid that some actual de-buggering is called for in this instance, or it will bite us harder later down the road :disappointed:  We don't think it's a flake b/c of an external dependency (or it would fail outside of Ubuntu) :confused: Brent's guess is something relating to AppArmor...which seems plausible but needs checking.
